---
title: "Basic Plots - Thesis"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-04-13"
---
This is some first exploration of the dataset and some basic plots/figures


\textbf{Dataset Summary and basic results}

```{r}

# Packages

library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)


# Read dataset 

data <- read_csv("C:/Users/Sofoklis/Documents/MATHS/Year 3/Block 4/Thesis/heart.csv")

# Save the plots

fig_path <- "C:/Users/Sofoklis/Documents/MATHS/Year 3/Block 4/Thesis/figures"

if (!dir.exists(fig_path)) {
  dir.create(fig_path)
}
```



```{r}
# Convert categorical variables 

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    Sex = factor(Sex),
    ChestPainType = factor(ChestPainType),
    FastingBS = factor(FastingBS),
    RestingECG = factor(RestingECG),
    ExerciseAngina = factor(ExerciseAngina),
    ST_Slope = factor(ST_Slope),
    HeartDisease = factor(HeartDisease)
  )


# Basic checks

dim(data)
glimpse(data)
summary(data)
colnames(data)
colSums(is.na(data))
cat("Number of rows containing a zero in the Cholesterol variable: ",
    sum(data$Cholesterol == 0)) 

# TABLE 1: Dataset dimensions

dataset_table <- data.frame(
  Observations = nrow(data),
  Variables = ncol(data)
)

kable(dataset_table, caption = "Dataset Dimensions")


# TABLE 2: Variable types

variable_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(data),
  Type = sapply(data, class)
)

kable(variable_table, caption = "Variable Types")


# TABLE 3: Missing values

missing_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(data),
  Missing = colSums(is.na(data))
)

kable(missing_table, caption = "Missing Values")


# TABLE 4: Summary statistics

numeric_summary <- data %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      everything(),
      list(
        Min = min,
        Q1 = ~ quantile(.x, 0.25),
        Median = median,
        Mean = mean,
        Q3 = ~ quantile(.x, 0.75),
        Max = max
      ),
      .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
    )
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = c("Variable", "Statistic"),
    names_sep = "_(?=[^_]+$)"   # FIXED split
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Statistic,
    values_from = value
  )

kable(numeric_summary, digits = 2,
      caption = "Summary Statistics for Numerical Variables")


# TABLE 5: Categorical variables

categorical_summary <- data %>%
  select(where(is.factor)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Category"
  ) %>%
  count(Variable, Category, name = "Count") %>%
  group_by(Variable) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * Count / sum(Count), 2)) %>%
  ungroup()

kable(categorical_summary,
      caption = "Distribution of Categorical Variables")


# TABLE 6: Response variable

response_table <- data %>%
  count(HeartDisease, name = "Count") %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * Count / sum(Count), 2))

kable(response_table,
      caption = "Distribution of Heart Disease")

```


\textbf{Plots for numerical variables}


```{r}
numeric_vars <- data %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  names()

for (var in numeric_vars) {
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[var]])) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "black", fill = "#4C72B0") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Distribution of", var),
      x = var,
      y = "Frequency"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(fig_path, paste0(var, "_distribution.png")),
    plot = p,
    width = 6,
    height = 4,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```

\textbf{Plots for categorical variables}

```{r}
categorical_vars <- data %>%
  select(where(is.factor)) %>%
  select(-HeartDisease) %>%
  names()

for (var in categorical_vars) {
  
  plot_data <- data %>%
    count(.data[[var]]) %>%
    mutate(
      prop = n / sum(n),
      label = paste0(round(prop * 100, 1), "%")
    )
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = .data[[var]], y = n)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#4C72B0", color = "black") +
    geom_text(
      aes(y = n / 2, label = label),
      color = "white",
      size = 4
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Distribution of", var),
      x = var,
      y = "Count"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(fig_path, paste0(var, "_distribution.png")),
    plot = p,
    width = 6,
    height = 4,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```

\textbf{Response Variable}
```{r}
# Response Variable

heart_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = HeartDisease)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#4C72B0", color = "black") +
  geom_text(
    stat = "count",
    aes(
      y = after_stat(count / 2), 
      label = paste0(round(after_stat(count / sum(count) * 100), 1), "%")
    ),
    color = "white",
    size = 5
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Heart Disease",
    x = "Heart Disease",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

heart_plot

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(fig_path, "heart_disease_distribution.png"),
  plot = heart_plot,
  width = 6,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)
```


\textbf{Numerical Variables vs HeartDisease}

```{r}
# Numerical Variables vs HeartDisease

numeric_vars <- data %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  names()

for (var in numeric_vars) {
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = HeartDisease, y = .data[[var]])) +
    geom_boxplot(fill = "#4C72B0", color = "black") +
    labs(
      title = paste(var, "by Heart Disease Status"),
      x = "Heart Disease",
      y = var
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(fig_path, paste0(var, "_by_HeartDisease.png")),
    plot = p,
    width = 6,
    height = 4,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```

\textbf{Categrical Variables vs HeartDisease}

```{r}
# Categorical Variables vs HeartDisease

for (var in categorical_vars) {
  
  plot_data <- data %>%
    count(.data[[var]], HeartDisease) %>%
    group_by(.data[[var]]) %>%
    mutate(
      prop = n / sum(n),
      label = paste0(round(prop * 100, 1), "%")
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = .data[[var]], y = prop, fill = HeartDisease)) +
    geom_col(position = "fill", color = "black") +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = label),
      position = position_fill(vjust = 0.5),
      color = "white",
      size = 3.5
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(var, "vs Heart Disease"),
      x = var,
      y = "Proportion",
      fill = "Heart Disease"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(fig_path, paste0(var, "_vs_HeartDisease.png")),
    plot = p,
    width = 8,
    height = 5.5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```
